title: Mullin Masters the Kent Wind
title_candidates: Deal Me In: Mullin's Opening Statement
The 16th Hole Hands Out Its Verdict
Holes 9-12: Where the Round Was Won
theme: On a brutal Open-hosting links, David Mullin's mid-round purple patch separates him from a field punished by Royal Cinque Ports — most of all Alex Baker, whose round unravels in double and quintuple bogeys.
opening_hook: Open with Royal Cinque Ports as the real protagonist — a two-time Open venue baring its teeth on TEG's second visit — before pivoting to Mullin emerging from the wind with a four-hole stretch (9-12) that effectively decided the day.
foreshadow: Alex Baker's early bleed at 3-5 (b08) as a warning of the chaos to come on the back nine
Mullin drawing level for the Jacket at the 9th (b11) as the moment that flipped into his decisive 9-12 surge
Jon Baker's quiet slide to the bottom of the Spoon race at the 4th (b07), an unflashy early move that holds all day

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: No prior state — this is the opening round. Mullin drew level at the 11th (b12) and took outright command at the 16th (b09), finishing three points clear of Alex Baker.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: Mullin levelled with Alex Baker at the 9th (b11) for the gross lead, then pulled away as Baker's card detonated on the back nine — a five-shot cushion over Jon Baker by the close.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: Jon Baker dropped to the foot of the Spoon race as early as the 4th (b07) and never climbed out, ending the day four points adrift of Gregg Williams.

players.arc: Built the round on a 13-point burst from the 9th to the 12th (b04), levelled and then took both leads outright, and ended R1 with the Trophy and the Jacket — the only man to truly tame the wind.

Briefly level for the Jacket at the 6th (b13) before his round collapsed in stages: six shots gone across 3-5 (b08), a quad at the 11th (b02), and a brutal 10 at the par-5 16th (b01) that gifted Mullin the outright Trophy lead.
A quad-bogey 9 at the 3rd (b03) set the tone, and although he hung on to third in both competitions, he never recovered enough to threaten the front.

Slipped to the bottom of the Spoon race at the 4th (b07) and stayed there despite a tidy three-par run at 11-13 (b06) — a respectable Jacket runner-up finish masking a long day at the foot of the Stableford.

venue_notes: Royal Cinque Ports, Deal — TEG's second visit, and only the second time in Kent. A two-time Open host and one of the sternest links on the Kent coast, especially in wind: the scoring (a winning 40 Stableford) tells the story of the course as much as the golfers.
